project.title: Role of the sarcin-ricin loop of 23S rRNA in biogenesis of the 50S ribosomal subunit
project.projectDescription: In this work, we show that 23S rRNA lacking the SRL can be efficiently reconstituted into 50S subunits, which exhibit full peptidyl transferase (PT) activity. Using cryo-EM and proteomic methods, we also further characterize affinity-purified SRL particles isolated from cells. Collectively, these data suggest that the SRL is needed to promote the dissociation of several AFs during 50S subunit biogenesis in the cell.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: The protein pellet were boiled in 4 % SDS in 100 mM Tris pH 8.0, reduce and alkylated at 95°C for 5 min in 10 mM Tris(2-Carboxyethyl)Phosphine hydrochloride (TCEP) and 40 mM 2-chloroacetamide, and ran on a short 10% SDS-PAGE gel. The proteins were digested out of the gel using two additions of trypsin with a total 20 hour 37C incubation. The resulting peptides were cleaned on with C-18 STop And Go Extraction (STAGE) tips (PMID 12498253) using 40% (v/v) acetonitrile in 0.1% (v/v) formic acid as the elution buffer. The peptides were resuspended in Buffer A consisting of 0.5% acetonitrile, 0.1% formic acid in MS grade water and peptide concentration was estimated by UV absorbance at 205nm using a NanoDropOne (Thermo Scientific, A205nm scopes).
project.dataProcessingProtocol: The data was searched with MaxQuant version 2.4.3.0 (PMID27809316), using Uniprot’s Escherichia coli proteomes (UP000002032 and/or UP000000625) with common contaminant sequences.

Comment[Instrument]: timsTOF Pro 2 (Bruker Daltonics) coupled to NanoElute UHPLC (Bruker Daltonics)
Comment[Separation]: Aurora Series Gen 25 cm analytical column (IonOpticks)
Comment[AcquisitionMethod]: Parallel Accumulation-Serial Fragmentation (PASEF) scan mode for DDA acquisition.
Comment[FlowRateChromatogram]: analysis was performed at 0.30 μL/min flow rate.
Comment[GradientTime]: A 30 min gradient was run from 2% B to 12% B over 15 min, then to 33% B from 15 to 30 min, then to 95% B over 0.5 min, held at 95% B for 7.72 min.
Comment[CollisionEnergy]: The collision energy was ramped linearly as a function of mobility value from 27 eV at 1/k0 = 0.7 V*sec/cm2 to 55 eV at 1/k0 = 1.35 V*sec/cm2.
Comment[IonizationType]: The CaptiveSpray ionization source was operated at 1800 V capillary voltage, 3 L/min drying gas, and 180°C drying temperature.
